findings.message: Using weak hashing algorithms is security-sensitive. Cryptographic hash algorithms such as MD2, MD4, MD5, MD6, HAVAL-128, SHA-1, and RIPEMD are no longer considered secure due to collision vulnerabilities. Use SHA-256, SHA-384, SHA-512, or SHA-3 instead.
